does that mean she will not have to go to bealrus

--- dkiesq@aol.com wrote:

Fyi
Sent from my BlackBerry® wireless handheld

-----Original Message-----
From: "Arda Beskardes" beskardes@gmail.com
Date: Mon, 15 May 2006 18:37:43
To:"DKIESQ@aol.com" DKIESQ@aol.com
Subject: Tatyana's case

Darren,

This is to confirm our telephone conversation of
today: Cancelling
her appointment will not render Tatyana out of
status. Her extension
of status is granted for three years and she is
legal to stay in the
US until the end of these three years. Therefore,
even when her visa
in her passportpires expires, she will still be
legal to remain in the
US. However, after her visa in her passport
expires, when she leaves
the country, she will need to apply for a new visa
to return.
Otherwise, she is completely in status and it was
not necessary for
her to rush to Paris to apply for the visa anyhow,
it was just a
decision of convenience.

Arda

--
